IDirectSoundStream::SetFormat

Changes the data format of the stream without releasing and recreating any objects.

HRESULT SetFormat(
    LPCWAVEFORMATEX pwfxFormat
);

Parameters

pwfxFormat
Address of a WAVEFORMATEX or WAVEFORMATEXTENSIBLE structure that describes the new format for the DirectSound stream.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful, an error code otherwise.

Remarks

The SetFormat method has the following restrictions:

  • This function stops the voice if it is playing. All pending packets are also flushed.
  • If the number of hardware voices has to change (1 hardware voice per 2 channels), the voice resources are freed and reallocated. Because of this, SetFormat may fail due to insufficient voices or buffer SGEs.

  • SetFormat always resets mixbin assignments. Calling SetFormat with a WAVEFORMATEXTENSIBLE structure that has its dwChannelMask member filled in will reset the mixbins to the channels supplied in the format.

    Because SetFormat resets the mixbin assignments, it should be called before calling SetOutputBuffer or SetMixBins. Calling SetOutputBuffer before calling SetFormat will cause SetFormat to add the default speaker mixbins to the original buffer. Similarly, calling SetMixBins before calling SetFormat will reset the mixbins to the default speaker mixbins.

  • 3D voices must be mono.

Requirements

Header: Declared in DSound.h.

Import Library: Use DSound.lib.
